How Could It Hurt?: Scope creep; the Aug 2026 demo slips; burnout
Existing Controls: Phased plan with documented non-goals; Phase 1 already built and passing tests
Additional Mitigation: Freeze the demo to Phase 1; hold milestones; defer the web GUI, DOOM sim, and M6

Risk: Facility review blocks the software inside
How Could It Hurt?: The primary channel (inside the wall) closes; can't reach the core audience
Existing Controls: Air-gapped single static binary; no network, no crypto; vocab-lint gate; runs on the walled-garden laptops facilities already deploy
Additional Mitigation: Get a DOC/facility reviewer to look early; ship a plain-language review packet; pilot with one facility

Risk: Simulator can't convincingly reach the DOOM finale
How Could It Hurt?: The payoff and differentiator weakens; late rework
Existing Controls: First sim slice built and tested (bus + panel + boot splash); SpiBus trait seam; doomgeneric is a known-portable target
Additional Mitigation: Prototype DOOM-on-sim-panel as an early vertical slice; keep a simpler bootable demo as fallback

How Could It Hurt?: Wrong teaching; lost credibility; support burden
Existing Controls: Unit tests across the workspace; self-validating check bank (tests assert every answer); fmt / clippy / vocab-lint in CI
Additional Mitigation: Mentor / expert review of lessons; grow coverage; user-acceptance test with a pilot learner

Risk: Learners get discouraged and drop out
How Could It Hurt?: Weak completion and outcomes undercut the whole case
Existing Controls: Self-paced and mastery-based; cannot get stuck; early visible payoff; progress is tracked
Additional Mitigation: Small peer cohort plus a mentor; tiny early wins; measure completion in a pilot

Risk: First-patch step overwhelms beginners; low-quality patches
How Could It Hurt?: Discouragement at the most important moment; reputational cost
Existing Controls: Start small (docs, staging); plug into kernelnewbies / LKMP / Outreachy; meritocracy framing
Additional Mitigation: Internal review before any patch is sent; a scaffolded first-issue list; a buddy for each first patch

Risk: No sponsor or funding to sustain / equip open-build learners
How Could It Hurt?: Hardware out of reach for outside learners; program longevity
Existing Controls: Free and open source; simulator-first means a zero-hardware floor; the $200 Saleae writeup bounty is collectable now
Additional Mitigation: Apply for grants; Saleae and partner outreach; keep the course fully usable with no hardware at all

How Could It Hurt?: The project stalls; the lived-experience voice is lost
Existing Controls: Specs, plans, and code are open source and documented (MIT / Apache-2.0)
Additional Mitigation: Grow co-maintainers out of the community; keep a written knowledge base
